steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Multiply equation 1 by 5 to match equation 2's left side: $$5(x + 6y) = 5(-14) \implies 5x + 30y = -70$$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Equation 2 says $5x + 30y = 0$, but equation 1 gives $5x + 30y = -70$. The same expression can't equal both 0 and −70 — the system is contradictory, so no solution exists.
